title: oenocarpus makeru
intro: Oenocarpus makeru, also known as the Maire's tamanu or makeru, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of New Caledonia. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin irritations and wounds due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits of Oenocarpus makeru include its ability to accelerate wound healing, reduce pain and inflammation, and promote skin regeneration. The plant's oil can be extracted and used in herbal preparations, such as topical creams and ointments, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall skin health.
